locked_parameters.graph/SWIG: single-treated-unit panel with donors j=1,...,J, pre periods t<=T0, and post period s
locked_parameters.treatment: D_0s=1, D_jt=0 for all donor-period cells and D_0t=0 for t<=T0
locked_parameters.target estimand: Q^0_0s(.) and F^0_0s(.), the untreated counterfactual quantile function and CDF for the treated unit at s
locked_parameters.identifying assumptions: consistency/no anticipation, donor untreated observation, rank-invariant interactive quantile factor law, finite-donor/pre-period observability, monotone quantile regularity, and finite-donor span/hull restrictions as stated below

named_assumptions.label: ass:consistency
named_assumptions.statement: For \(t\le T_0\), the observed treated distribution equals \(F^0_{0t}\). For every donor \(j\) and every \(t\le s\), the observed donor distribution equals \(F^0_{jt}\). The treated post-period observed distribution may differ from \(F^0_{0s}\), and no donor unit is affected by the treated unit's intervention.

named_assumptions.label: ass:monotone
named_assumptions.statement: For every unit-period untreated cell, \(Q^0_{it}\) is finite a.e., left-continuous, and nondecreasing in \(u\). Candidate counterfactual quantile functions must themselves be finite a.e., left-continuous, and nondecreasing in \(u\); pointwise compatible selections that violate monotonicity are not admissible members of \(\mathcal{Q}_I(P)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:qif
named_assumptions.statement: There is a finite \(R\) such that for a.e. \(u\in(0,1)\), \(Q^0_{it}(u)=m_t(u)+\ell_t(u)^\transpose b_i\) for all untreated unit-period cells, with unit loading \(b_i\) fixed across both periods and ranks. The rank index \(u\) is common across the untreated potential-outcome path; no restriction is imposed on the copula between \(Y_{0s}(1)\) and \(Y_{0s}(0)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:finiteobs
named_assumptions.statement: The observed law \(P\) contains the exact donor quantile functions \(Q^0_{jt}\) for \(j=1,\ldots,J\) and \(t\le s\), and the exact treated pre-period quantile functions \(Q^0_{0t}\) for \(t\le T_0\). Sampling error and estimated quantile error are outside this note.

named_assumptions.label: ass:rank
named_assumptions.statement: For a.e. \(u\), the donor loading matrix has affine rank at least \(\min(R,J-1)\), and the donor pre-period quantile matrix is not collapsed to a constant vector. This excludes degenerate observable laws where every donor has the same quantile path.

named_assumptions.label: ass:exactfit
named_assumptions.statement: The treated pre-period vector \(x_0(u)\) belongs to the affine hull of the donor pre-period vectors generated by the same rank-\(u\) factor law for a.e. \(u\), and these rank-wise fits admit a common set of unit loadings across ranks. Equivalently, the global compatibility set \(\mathcal{B}(P)\) is nonempty.

named_assumptions.label: ass:convexfit
named_assumptions.statement: For a.e. \(u\), there exists at least one weight vector \(w(u)\in\Delta^{J-1}\) such that \(x_0(u)=X(u)w(u)\). This is stronger than Assumption~\ref{ass:exactfit} and is used for convex donor-weight claims.

named_assumptions.label: ass:postconstant
named_assumptions.statement: For a.e. \(u\), the value \(q_B(u)=m_s(u)+\ell_s(u)^\transpose b_0\) is constant over all monotone-admissible global compatible systems \(B\in\mathcal{B}^m(P)\). Systems in \(\mathcal{B}(P)\) whose post-period path is not a valid finite left-continuous nondecreasing quantile function are excluded from this invariance condition.

named_assumptions.label: ass:affineinterior
named_assumptions.statement: On a positive-measure set of ranks, \(\mathcal{W}_C(P,u)\) has nonempty relative interior inside \(\mathcal{W}_A(P,u)\). This condition rules out boundary-only convex fits where every feasible perturbation immediately leaves the donor simplex.

named_assumptions.label: ass:topology
named_assumptions.statement: All open-set claims for observable panels are stated in the product \(L^\infty\) topology \(d_\infty\) on \(\mathfrak{P}\), relative to the subspace of panels satisfying the finite-donor observability, nondegenerate-rank, exact-fit, convex-fit, and affine-interior restrictions. Equality restrictions such as exact pre-period fit define the ambient subspace for relative openness.

named_assumptions.label: ass:mean-shape
named_assumptions.statement: There is an integrable nondecreasing base quantile function \(H:(0,1)\to\R\), constants \(\alpha_i\), and period shifts \(\lambda_t\) such that \(Q^0_{it}(u)=H(u)+\alpha_i+\lambda_t\) for all untreated unit-period cells and a.e. \(u\).

estimand_functional: P \mapsto \mathcal{Q}_I(P)=\{q_B:B\in\mathcal{B}^m(P)\}; donor-weight reductions use \underline q_C(u),\overline q_C(u) from the explicit J-variable LPs and the row-span map \bar X(u)^\transpose a(u)=z(u).

conjectures.label: point-boundary
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency}--\ref{ass:exactfit}, \(Q^0_{0s}\) is point identified by \(P\) if and only if Assumption~\ref{ass:postconstant} holds a.e. in \(u\).  In the donor-weight submodel of Definition~\ref{def:donorweight}, this is equivalent to \(z(u)\in\operatorname{rowspan}\bar X(u)\) a.e.; under Assumption~\ref{ass:convexfit}, in the convex donor-weight submodel, the sharp quantile identified set is exactly the monotone LP envelope generated by the rankwise programs \(\underline q_C(u)\) and \(\overline q_C(u)\), and the sharp CDF bounds are
\[
\underline F_{DW,C}(y)=\inf_{q\in\mathcal{Q}_{DW,C}(P)}\int_0^1\one\{q(u)\le y\}\,du,\qquad
\overline F_{DW,C}(y)=\sup_{q\in\mathcal{Q}_{DW,C}(P)}\int_0^1\one\{q(u)\le y\}\,du.
\]
If \(z(u)\notin\operatorname{rowspan}\bar X(u)\) on a positive-measure set and Assumptions~\ref{ass:rank} and \ref{ass:affineinterior} hold, then \(\mathcal{Q}_{DW}(P)\) contains at least two distinct monotone compatible quantile functions and \(\mathcal{F}_{DW}(P)\) is non-singleton.  The stable convex-loading class in Theorem~\ref{thm:convex-sufficient} is therefore a strict point-identified subclass of the rank-invariant finite-donor quantile-factor class whenever the global post-period functional is not invariant; in the donor-weight submodel, augmented row-span failure is the observable certificate for that noninvariance.

conjectures.label: escape
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency}, \ref{ass:monotone}, \ref{ass:qif}, \ref{ass:topology}, \ref{ass:rank}, \ref{ass:exactfit}, \ref{ass:convexfit}, and \ref{ass:affineinterior}, there is a nonempty relatively \(d_\infty\)-open set of observable donor/pre-period/post-donor quantile panels \(P\) for which the post-period donor quantile vector \(z(u)\notin\operatorname{rowspan}\bar X(u)\) on a positive-measure set of ranks.  Equivalently, there is an affine null direction \(h(u)\) with \(\one^\transpose h(u)=0\), \(X(u)h(u)=0\), and \(z(u)^\transpose h(u)\ne0\).  On this relative open set, two convex-compatible donor-weight systems that admit global rank-invariant quantile-factor representations agree on \(P\) but imply different monotone \(Q^0_{0s}\), so point identification fails even when a convex distributional synthetic-control weight vector fits all pre-period quantiles.

supporting_theorems.label: sharp-set
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency}--\ref{ass:exactfit}, any \(q\in\mathcal{Q}_I(P)\) is attainable as \(Q^0_{0s}\) by some data-generating process satisfying the rank-invariant interactive quantile-factor law and matching the observed law \(P\).  Conversely, every data-generating process satisfying those assumptions and matching \(P\) has \(Q^0_{0s}\in\mathcal{Q}_I(P)\).  Therefore \(\mathcal{Q}_I(P)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_I(P)\) are the sharp identified sets for the treated unit's untreated post-period quantile function and CDF.

supporting_theorems.label: convex-sufficient
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency}--\ref{ass:rank} and Assumption~\ref{ass:convexfit}, suppose there is a single weight vector \(w\in\Delta^{J-1}\), independent of \(u\), such that \(Q^0_{0t}(u)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_jQ^0_{jt}(u)\) for all pre-periods and a.e. \(u\), and suppose this same \(w\) represents the treated loading \(b_0=\sum_j w_jb_j\) in every compatible factorization.  Then Assumption~\ref{ass:postconstant} holds and \(Q^0_{0s}(u)=\sum_j w_jQ^0_{js}(u)\) a.e.

substrate_survey.method: lean_local_search only; no source files read
bypass_justified: Identifiable and standard potential-outcome structures are at a different abstraction level from this deterministic rank-wise quantile-panel compatibility theorem.
Adjustment, front-door, instrument, dynamic-regime, and graphical primitives do not match the locked parameter tuple because there are no covariates, mediators, instruments, or sequential treatments.
The active proof obligations are finite-dimensional row-space/nullspace identities, linear programs, and monotone quantile selections, all stated in the TeX setup.
